  • ❓ Why DTA Pro?

Study Data Entry

Enter 2x2 table data: TP (True Positives), FP (False Positives), FN (False Negatives), TN (True Negatives). Studies with zero cells will be flagged. Covariates can be added for meta-regression.

Model Settings

Model Selection Guidance:
• Auto (AIC-stacked): Fits both models, uses AIC weights, and averages when no single model dominates
• Bivariate GLMM: Preferred when pooling sensitivity/specificity at a common threshold (Macaskill et al. 2010)
• HSROC: Preferred when studies use different thresholds or exploring threshold effects (Harbord et al. 2007)
• Both models are mathematically equivalent when no covariates (Harbord et al. 2007)
If best AIC weight is below this threshold, auto mode reports model-averaged estimates.
Default 0.5 correction per Cochrane Handbook for Systematic Reviews of DTA (Macaskill et al. 2023, Ch. 10.4.3)

Analysis Options

Method: Non-parametric resampling with bias-corrected and accelerated (BCa) intervals.
Ref: Efron B, Tibshirani RJ. An Introduction to the Bootstrap. 1993.

QUADAS-2 Quality Assessment

QUADAS-2 assesses risk of bias across 4 domains and applicability concerns across 3 domains (Patient Selection, Index Test, Reference Standard). Complete the assessment for each study in the Data Input tab.

Advanced Statistical Diagnostics

Comprehensive diagnostic tools including outlier detection, threshold effect analysis, prediction intervals, and GRADE-DTA certainty assessment.

Run analysis to view advanced diagnostics

Outlier & Influential Study Detection

Based on Viechtbauer & Cheung (2010) and Matsushima et al. (2020). Identifies studies with unusual results or disproportionate influence on pooled estimates.

StudySR (Sens)SR (Spec)Cook's DDFBETAS SensDFBETAS SpecStatus

Threshold Effect Analysis

Spearman correlation between sensitivity and false positive rate to detect threshold variability.

Youden Index (J Statistic)

Moses-Littenberg Symmetric SROC

Original symmetric SROC model (Moses et al. 1993) for comparison with HSROC.

Prediction Intervals

95% prediction intervals for sensitivity and specificity in a new study setting, accounting for between-study heterogeneity.

Partial AUC Analysis

AUC restricted to clinically relevant FPR ranges (e.g., 0-20% for screening tests).

GRADE-DTA Certainty of Evidence

Semi-automated assessment based on GRADE guidelines for diagnostic test accuracy reviews.

Fagan Nomogram (Clinical Probability Revision)

Based on Fagan (1975). Shows how test results change disease probability.

ROC Space Regions (Reitsma 2005)

95% confidence region (where true summary point lies) and 95% prediction region (where future studies may fall).

Crosshairs Plot

Individual study CIs displayed as crosshairs in ROC space. Point size proportional to study weight.

Likelihood Ratio Analysis

Decision Curve Analysis (Vickers 2006)

Net benefit analysis comparing test-guided decisions vs. treat-all/treat-none strategies.

Information Gain (Shannon Entropy)

Quantifies diagnostic uncertainty reduction in bits of information.

Extended Heterogeneity Statistics

Comprehensive heterogeneity assessment with confidence intervals.

Publication Bias Tests (Harbord 2006, Peters 2006)

DTA-specific small-study effect tests (Harbord/Peters, require >=10), selection-function SROC sensitivity, and likelihood-based selection modeling with bootstrap uncertainty.

Used for uncertainty intervals in the likelihood-based selection SROC model. Changing this re-runs the likelihood model automatically.

Number Needed to Diagnose (NND)

Clinical interpretability metrics: NND (1/Youden), PSI, NNM, and LDM (Bandos 2010).

Galbraith (Radial) Plot

Standardized effect vs precision plot for heterogeneity visualization (Galbraith 1988).

Pooled Diagnostic Odds Ratio (DOR)

Random effects DOR with comprehensive heterogeneity statistics (Q, H, I², τ²).

Diagnostic Accuracy Indices

Matthews CC, F1 Score, Balanced Accuracy, Discriminant Power (Powers 2011).

Approximate Bayesian Posterior

Bayesian estimates using conjugate normal-normal model with weakly informative prior.

Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation

LOO-CV statistics with ELPD and influence diagnostics (Vehtari 2017).

Sensitivity Analysis Summary

Summary of robustness across different sensitivity analyses.

Cutting-Edge Statistical Methods

Beyond R Packages
These advanced methods exceed the capabilities of standard R packages (mada, diagmeta, metafor). They are based on the latest methodological research and validated simulation studies.

Multiple Thresholds Meta-Analysis

Hoyer (2018) method for handling multiple thresholds per study - validated in 2024 simulation study as superior to ignoring threshold multiplicity. Works with standard 2×2 data and models within/between-study threshold correlation.

Advanced Heterogeneity Measures

Median Odds Ratio, Prediction Ellipse Area, and Bivariate I² - metrics missing from 90% of Cochrane DTA reviews.

Firth Correction for Sparse Data

Penalized likelihood estimation that avoids continuity correction bias for zero cells. Superior to +0.5 correction.

Robust Variance Estimation

Sandwich (Huber-White) estimators for model-robust standard errors even under misspecification.

Sequential Testing Analysis

Model test combinations considering conditional dependence between sequential tests.

Relative Diagnostic Odds Ratio

rDOR for comparing two tests directly - more interpretable than difference in SROC AUC.

Profile Likelihood Confidence Intervals

Exact CIs based on profile likelihood - more accurate than Wald intervals for small samples and extreme proportions.

Influence Diagnostics & Leave-One-Out

Cook's distance, DFBETAS, and COVRATIO for bivariate model - identify influential studies affecting pooled estimates.

Cumulative Meta-Analysis

Chronological accumulation of evidence - detect when conclusions became stable.

LASSO Meta-Regression

L1-penalized regression for automatic covariate selection in meta-regression. Requires studies with covariates (year, quality score, or custom covariates).

Small-Study Effects Meta-Regression

Debray's approach - regress DOR on precision to detect publication bias.

Prediction Interval Inference

95% prediction intervals for future studies - more meaningful than CIs for heterogeneous data.

Optimal Threshold Determination

Find the operating point that maximizes Youden's J, minimizes cost, or optimizes clinical utility.

Bivariate Contour-Enhanced Funnel Plot

Simultaneous assessment of publication bias in sensitivity and specificity with significance contours.

R Script Export

Generate reproducible R code for mada, metafor, and diagmeta packages. Run in R/RStudio to independently verify results.

          WebR: Validate with R in Your Browser
        

        

          Not loaded
          
        

      

      

        

          Independent Verification: This loads a full R environment directly in your browser using
          WebR (WebAssembly).
          It runs a bivariate random-effects model on the current dataset and compares results with DTA Pro's JavaScript engine.
          
First load: ~10-30 seconds (downloads ~20MB R runtime). Subsequent runs are cached.
          
No data leaves your browser. All computation is local.

Statistical Methods Appendix

        Complete Mathematical Reference
      

      

        

          Reference Guide: Complete mathematical formulations for all statistical methods in DTA Pro v4.9 (PLOS ONE Edition)
        


        
        

          
1. Bivariate Random-Effects Model (Reitsma)

          

            
Joint analysis of sensitivity and specificity with correlation.

            

              logit(sensi) = mu_A + u_Ai

              logit(speci) = mu_B + u_Bi

              Pooled Sens = expit(mu_A) = 1/(1 + exp(-mu_A))

              Pooled Spec = expit(mu_B)
            

            
Ref: Reitsma JB et al. (2005) J Clin Epidemiol 58:982-990

          

        


        
        

          
2. HSROC Model

          

            

              Lambda = accuracy parameter

              Theta = threshold parameter

              beta = asymmetry parameter

              SROC: sens = f(1-spec, Lambda, beta)
            

            
Ref: Rutter CM, Gatsonis CA (2001) Stat Med 20:2865-2884

          

        


        
        

          
3. Heterogeneity Statistics

          

            

              Q = Sum(wi*(yi - theta)^2)

              I^2 = max(0, (Q - df)/Q) * 100%

              tau^2 = (Q - (k-1)) / C

              Prediction: theta +/- t * sqrt(tau^2 + SE^2)
            

            
Ref: Higgins JPT et al. (2003) BMJ 327:557-560

          

        


        
        

          
4. Confidence Interval Methods

          

            

              

                Clopper-Pearson: Exact Beta quantiles
              

              

                Wilson: Score interval
              

              

                Agresti-Coull: Adjusted Wald
              

              

                HKSJ: t-distribution with k-1 df
              

            

          

        


        
        

          
5. Minimum Number of Studies (k) Guidelines

          

            

              Important: Reliable DTA meta-analysis requires sufficient studies for stable estimation.
            

            

              
                

                  k
                  Analysis Type
                  Recommendation
                

              
              
                
k = 1Study-level onlyMeta-analysis not supported

                
k = 2Minimal poolingConsider narrative synthesis; results highly uncertain

                
k = 3-4Basic poolingHKSJ correction applied; heterogeneity estimates unstable

                
k = 5-9Standard analysisAdequate for point estimates; limited power for heterogeneity

                
k ≥ 10Full analysisReliable for all analyses including publication bias tests

              
            

            

              Ref: Cochrane DTA Handbook; Takwoingi Y et al. (2019) Stat Med 38:1998-2010
            

          

        


        
        

          
6. Publication Bias Tests

          

            

              Deeks: lnDOR ~ 1/sqrt(ESS), p < 0.10

              Harbord: Z/sqrt(V) ~ (1/sqrt(V)) + lnDOR

              Peters: lnDOR ~ 1/N, weighted by ESS

              Egger: Z/SE ~ 1/SE + effect
            

          

        


        
        

          
7. Clinical Utility Measures

          

            

              LR+ = Sens / (1 - Spec)

              LR- = (1 - Sens) / Spec

              DOR = (TP*TN) / (FP*FN)

              Post-test prob = (PreOdds * LR) / (1 + PreOdds * LR)

              NND = 1 / (Sens - (1 - Spec))

Multi-Model Ensemble

        

          

            
Ensemble Meta-Analysis

          

          

            
Combines 4 models (Bivariate, HSROC, Copula, Exact Binomial) using inverse-variance weighting for robust estimates.

Beyond R: Advanced Methods

        Impossible in Standard R Packages
      

      

        

          These methods would require hundreds of lines of custom R code or combining multiple packages.
          Here they are available in one click with interactive visualizations.
        

        
Core Advanced Methods

        

          

            
Exact Binomial GLMM

            

              
True binomial likelihood without normal approximation. More accurate for small samples.

              
              

            

          

          

            
Bayesian Model Averaging

            

              
Average across bivariate, HSROC, and copula models weighted by posterior probability.

              
              

            

          

          

            
Prior Sensitivity Analysis

            

              
Test how different prior choices affect Bayesian estimates.

              
              

            

          

        

        
Clinical Decision Tools

        

          

            
Decision Curve Analysis

            

              
Net benefit across threshold probabilities - clinical utility visualization.

              

                
                
              

              
              

            

          

          

            
Interactive LR Nomogram

            

              
Fagan nomogram with draggable pre-test probability.

              

                
                
              

              

            

          

          

            
Cost-Effectiveness Calculator

            

              
QALY-based analysis with willingness-to-pay threshold.

              

                

                  
                  
                

                

                  
                  
                

              

              
              

            

          

        

        
Power & Sample Size

        

          

            
Sample Size Calculator

            

              
How many studies needed for desired precision?

              

                

                  
                  
                

                

                  
                  
                

              

              
              

            

          

          

            
Power Analysis

            

              
Statistical power of current meta-analysis to detect effects.

              

                

                  
                  
                

                

                  
                  
                

              

              
              

            

          

        

        
Heterogeneity Deep Dive

        

          

            
Heterogeneity Decomposition

            

              
Partition variance by threshold, population, and unexplained.

              
              

            

          

          

            
Evidence Stability Analysis

            

              
When did the evidence become mature enough to trust?

Validate model fit using cross-validation.

              

                
                
              

              
              

            

          

        

        
Test Comparison & Ranking

        

          

            
Network Ranking (P-score/SUCRA)

            

              
Rank multiple tests by probability of being best (like SUCRA for treatment NMA).

              
              

            

          

          

            
Advanced Threshold Effect Model

            

              
Moses-Littenberg with heterogeneous thresholds per study.

Per-Test DTA Comparison (Independent Analyses)

        
      

      

        

          Compare multiple diagnostic tests simultaneously using network meta-analysis principles.
          Allows indirect comparisons when direct head-to-head data is limited.
        

        

          
          
        

        

        

        
        
        

          
Enter multi-test study data and click "Run Network Analysis"

Report Preview

          

        

      

    

  

    

      

        

          

            
Per-Test DTA Comparison (Independent Analyses per Test)

          

          

            

              Compare multiple diagnostic tests simultaneously using network meta-analysis principles.
              Requires data from studies comparing 2+ tests against the same reference standard.
            

            

              
              
            

            

            

              
              
            

            

          

        

        

          

            
IPD-DTA (Individual Patient Data)

          

          

            

              Analyze individual patient-level data for more precise estimates and subgroup analyses.
              Accounts for clustering within studies.
            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            
            
            

          

        

        

          

            
Bayesian Bivariate Model (MCMC)

          

          

            

              Full Bayesian estimation using Markov Chain Monte Carlo. Provides posterior distributions
              and credible intervals. More appropriate for small sample sizes.
            

            

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

            

            
            

            

          

        

        

          

            
Latent Class Models (Imperfect Reference)

          

          

            

              When no gold standard exists, use latent class analysis to estimate test accuracy
              assuming disease status is a latent (unobserved) variable.
            

            

              
              
            

            

            

              
              
            

            
            
            

          

        

        

          

            
Comparative Accuracy (Head-to-Head)

          

          

            

              Direct comparison of two tests applied to the same patients. Accounts for correlation
              between tests and provides relative DOR, sensitivity difference, and specificity difference.
            

            

              

                
Test A

                

              

              

                
Test B

                

              

            

            

              
              
            

            
            
            

          

        

        

          

            
Multiple Thresholds Analysis

          

          

            

              Analyze test accuracy across multiple cutoff values. Estimates a summary ROC curve
              accounting for threshold variability. Based on Steinhauser et al. approach.
            

            

              
              
            

            
            
            

            

          

        

        

          

            
Sequential Testing Models

          

          

            

              Model sequential/serial testing strategies (e.g., screening followed by confirmatory test).
              Calculates combined accuracy and optimal testing sequences.
            

            

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            
            

          

        

        

          

            
Test-Treatment Threshold Analysis

          

          

            

              Determine optimal testing and treatment thresholds based on clinical utility.
              Incorporates benefits/harms of true/false positives and negatives.
            

            

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

            

            
            

            

          

        

        

          

            
GRADE-DTA Certainty Assessment

          

          

            

              Assess certainty of evidence using the GRADE approach adapted for diagnostic test accuracy.
              Based on Schunemann et al. 2020 framework.
            

            
            

          

        

        

          

            
Sensitivity Analysis Suite

          

          

            

              Comprehensive robustness checks including leave-one-out, model comparison,
              continuity correction sensitivity, and outlier detection.
            

            

              
              
            

            

          

        

        

          

            
Methodological References

          

          

            

              Key citations for methods used in this analysis. Include in your manuscript's methods section.
            

            
            

          

        

        

          

            
Living Meta-Analysis (Sequential Monitoring)

            Beyond R
          

          

            

              O'Brien-Fleming alpha-spending for continuous evidence monitoring.
              Enables early stopping while controlling Type I error.
            

            

              
              
            

            
            

            

          

        

        

          

            
ML-Enhanced Outlier Detection

            Beyond R
          

          

            

              Isolation Forest + Local Outlier Factor algorithms.
              More robust than traditional statistical methods.
            

            
            

          

        

        

          

            
Simulation-Based Power Analysis

            Beyond R
          

          

            

              Monte Carlo simulation for sample size planning in DTA meta-analysis.
            

            

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

            

            
            

          

        

        

          

            
Patient-Level Microsimulation

            Beyond R
          

          

            

              Simulate individual patient outcomes to estimate clinical and economic impact.
            

            

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

            

            
            

          

        

        

          

            
Bayesian Model Averaging

            Beyond R
          

          

            

              Combine estimates across multiple models weighted by posterior probability.
              Accounts for model uncertainty in final estimates.
            

            
            

          

        

        

          

            
Temporal Trend Analysis

            Beyond R
          

          

            

              Analyze how diagnostic accuracy has changed over time.
              Requires year data in covariate field.
            

            
            

            

          

        

        

          

            
Clinical Scenario Builder

            Beyond R
          

          

            

              What-if analysis: explore how different assumptions affect clinical utility.
            

            
            

          

        

        

          

            
PRISMA-DTA Flow Diagram

            Beyond R
          

          

            

              Generate publication-ready PRISMA-DTA flow diagram as SVG.
            

            
            

          

        

      

    



  

    
Advanced Clinical Tools

    +
  

  

    

      
Decision Curve Analysis

      
Net benefit across threshold probabilities - Vickers method

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      
      

    

    

      
Interactive Fagan Nomogram

      
Pre-test to post-test probability visualization

      

        

          
          
          20%
        

      

      
      

    

    

      
Test Sequence Optimizer

      
Optimal ordering of serial/parallel testing strategies

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      
      

    

    

      
Expected Value of Perfect Information (EVPI)

      
Value of eliminating decision uncertainty - guides research priorities

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      
      

    

  





  

    
Bias Correction Methods

    +
  

  

    

      
Verification Bias Correction

      
Adjust for partial/differential verification - Begg-Greenes method

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      
      

    

    

      
Spectrum Effect Analysis

      
How disease severity affects diagnostic accuracy

      
      

    

    

      
Selection Model Bias Adjustment

      
Copas-style selection model for DTA - adjusts for selective reporting

      

        

          
          
        

      

      
      

    

  





  

    
Population Impact Analysis

    +
  

  

    

      
Diagnostic Yield Calculator

      
Population-level impact of testing strategy

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      
      

    

    

      
Number Needed to Test/Diagnose

      
Clinical efficiency metrics at different prevalences

      
      

    

    

      
Net Reclassification Improvement (NRI)

      
Compare classification performance between tests

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      
      

    

  





  

    
Advanced Visualizations

    +
  

  

    

      
Interactive ROC Explorer

      
Click on SROC curve to see threshold-specific metrics

      
      

    

    

      
Heterogeneity Source Heatmap

      
Visual decomposition of heterogeneity by study characteristics

      
      

    

    

      
Covariate Bubble Plot

      
ROC space with size/color mapped to study covariates

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      
      

    

    

      
Crosshairs Plot

      
Study-level confidence regions in ROC space

      
      

    

  





  

    
Threshold Optimization Tools

    +
  

  

    

      
Optimal Threshold Finder

      
Find cutoff maximizing clinical utility (Youden, cost-weighted, or custom)

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      
      

    

    

      
Rule-In / Rule-Out Threshold Analyzer

      
SpPin (high specificity) and SnNout (high sensitivity) thresholds

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      
      

    

    

      
Treatment Threshold Calculator

      
Pauker-Kassirer test-treatment threshold analysis

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      
      

    

  





  

    
Health Technology Assessment

    +
  

  

    

      
Cost-Effectiveness Analysis

      
ICER calculation with willingness-to-pay threshold

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      
      

    

    

      
Budget Impact Analysis

      
Population-level budget impact modeling

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      
      

    

    

      
Cost-Effectiveness Acceptability Curve

      
Probability of cost-effectiveness across WTP thresholds

      
      

    

    

      
Tornado Diagram (One-Way Sensitivity)

      
Identify most influential parameters

      
      

    

  





  

    
Influence Diagnostics

    +
  

  

    

      
Cook's Distance for DTA

      
Measure each study's influence on pooled estimates

      
      

    

    

      
DFBETAS Analysis

      
Study influence on individual parameters

      
      

    

    

      
Comprehensive Outlier Detection

      
Multiple methods: standardized residuals, Mahalanobis, influence

      
      

    

  





  

    
Publication Bias Suite

    +
  

  

    

      
Egger-like Regression Tests

      
Multiple regression approaches for asymmetry

      
      

    

    

      
Trim-and-Fill for DTA

      
Impute missing studies and adjust estimates

      
      

    

    

      
P-Curve Analysis

      
Evidential value assessment - detect p-hacking

      
      

    

    

      
PET-PEESE Small-Study Correction

      
Precision-effect regression correction on log-DOR (publication selection adjustment)

      
      

    

    

      
Excess Significance Test

      
Observed vs expected significant findings under estimated true effect

      
      

    

    

      
Exact Excess Significance (Poisson-Binomial)

      
Exact tail probability for observed significant findings under study-specific power model

      
      

    

    

      
Worst-Case Selection Bounds (Partial Identification)

      
Nonparametric monotone-selection envelope for pooled log-DOR under unknown publication probabilities

      
      

    

    

      
Significance Caliper Discontinuity Test

      
Tests for excess p-values just below alpha=0.05 versus just above (publication/p-hacking signal)

      
      

    

    

      
Selection-Ratio Sensitivity Curve

      
Evaluates pooled DOR under increasing publication preference for statistically significant studies

      
      

    

  





  

    
Meta-Regression Tools

    +
  

  

    

      
Covariate Interaction Explorer

      
Explore interactions between study-level covariates

      
      

    

    

      
Meta-Regression Coefficient Plot

      
Forest plot of regression coefficients with CIs

      
      

    

    

      
Meta-Regression Scatter

      
Accuracy vs continuous covariate with fitted line

      

        
        
      

      
      

    

  





  

    
Reporting Tools

    +
  

  

    

      
Publication-Ready Tables

      
Generate formatted tables for manuscripts

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      
      

    

    

      
Methods Section Generator

      
Auto-generate methods text for manuscripts

      
      

    

    

      
Results Section Generator

Cumulative Meta-Analysis Plot

      
How estimates evolved over time

      
      

    

    

      
Subgroup Comparison Forest

L'Abbé Plot for DTA

Galbraith (Radial) Plot
